response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and backend engineer with broad language exposure, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is marginal — limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows. His entire career trajectory has been web, mobile, and cloud-native systems using TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure, and no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency. The single tangential finance touch (RIDE Capital) was purely Node.js-based product work unrelated to trading systems or financial instruments.
strengths: CI/CD and DevOps familiarity — set up and maintained GitLab, CI servers, Docker, and Artifactory at Altom
Some Java background, which is listed as a desirable skill for the role
SQL listed as a skill with practical database usage across multiple roles (MongoDB, MySQL)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project) — nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge across 15+ years of career history
No Windows platform development experience; work history is predominantly Linux and cloud-native environments

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain exposure
